bulletins.title: Cold-Calling Fraudsters Target Seniors with Gold Bar Courier Scheme
bulletins.summary: Impersonators calling victims claim their financial accounts are at immediate risk and instruct them to remain on the phone while liquidating savings to buy gold bars. Scammers then dispatch a physical courier to the victim's home under the guise of transferring the gold to a safe vault.
phrases: your money is at immediate risk

bulletins.title: Fraudsters Impersonate FBI and IC3 Agents in Asset Recovery Scams
bulletins.summary: Scammers are contacting previous cybercrime victims via direct messages, posing as FBI or IC3 representatives. They claim to offer professional scam recovery services to trace transactions and retrieve lost funds, ultimately attempting to solicit money or personal credentials.

bulletins.title: Romance Scammers Leverage Fake Travel Crises for Wire Demands
bulletins.summary: Online romance scammers groom victims over messaging platforms like WhatsApp without speaking in person, then fabricate sudden emergencies to request urgent wire transfers. Script tactics involve claiming to be stranded, unable to buy plane tickets, or in immediate danger.
phrases: could not get a plane ticket

add_phrases: your money is at immediate risk
could not get a plane ticket

gemini_context_snippet: Active phone and messaging scam campaigns this week focus on government impersonation gold courier pickups, fake FBI IC3 recovery agents targeting past scam victims via direct messaging, and romance scammers fabricating travel emergencies. Warn callers if someone instructs them to liquidate savings into gold bars for courier collection or claims to recover stolen crypto/funds via social media DMs.
